title: task-pipeline config
description: Generic contract for a pipeline config. An ordered list of stages; each stage is run by the host project's own skills/agents and guarded by a typed gate. The framework imposes no specific stages, skills, or gate assignments — those are entirely the host project's config. Copy pipeline.example.json and rewrite it to match your project.

stages.description: The pipeline stages, in order. Any number, any names — your project's real stages.

release.description: Optional release automation, entirely project-defined and INDIVIDUALLY TOGGLEABLE. Omit the whole object, or set enabled:false, to turn release automation off for this project. The framework ships an example workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) whose job is gated on a repo variable so each project arms it on its own; this block is the declarative counterpart the orchestrator reads.

enabled.description: Master on/off toggle. false (or the object omitted) = no release automation for this project.

trigger.description: What kicks off a release (e.g. a pushed vX.Y.Z tag, manual dispatch).

steps.description: Ordered release actions, in prose — project-specific (e.g. create a GitHub release, npm publish). Human-only steps (2FA publish) are named as such.

verify.description: Post-release smoke checks that must pass after a release — this is the release's own post-deploy gate (stage 8 applied to shipping the package itself).

state.description: Unique stable key for the stage.

model.description: Optional recommended model id for the stage.

skills.description: The skill(s)/agent(s) that execute this stage. Any names your environment resolves — this is where you plug in your OWN skills.

gate.description: Condition that must pass before advancing to the next stage.

type.description: auto = the orchestrator verifies `check` itself (pass/fail); manual = wait for the operator's explicit go.
